summary: Recovered the live Keycloak password-reset path by moving realm SMTP to the VM-private Stalwart relay, repairing Docker nat-chain drift before container recreation, and pointing Keycloak at the reachable PostgreSQL primary on docker-runtime-lv3.

verification.observed: `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:19000/health/ready` on docker-runtime-lv3 returned `status: UP` with the database and cluster checks passing after the live replay.

A fresh browser-equivalent request chain from `https://ops.example.com/oauth2/sign_in` rendered the Keycloak reset form at `https://sso.example.com/realms/lv3/login-actions/reset-credentials?...` instead of the generic `Unexpected error when handling authentication request to identity provider.` page.
Posting `username=florin.badita` to the live reset action returned Keycloak's success banner, `You should receive an email shortly with further instructions.`
`docker ps` on docker-runtime-lv3 showed `lv3-mail-stalwart` publishing `10.10.10.20:1587->1587/tcp`, matching the repo-managed private submission relay used by Keycloak.

notes: Before the fix, the reset form either failed inside Keycloak with a generic identity-provider error or the SMTP send path failed because Keycloak did not trust the public Stalwart submission certificate chain for STARTTLS.

The live docker-runtime host still exhibits occasional missing `DOCKER` nat-chain state after daemon restarts, so the Keycloak and mail-platform roles now probe that condition and force-recreate containers only when recovery is required.

The repo's declared PostgreSQL HA VIP path for Keycloak was not reachable from docker-runtime-lv3 during this recovery, so Keycloak now follows `postgres_ha.initial_primary` until the VIP routing issue is fixed.
